arguments.task: Open the official iPayX documentation and site pages needed to determine: 1) what iPayX is in one sentence, 2) documented API authentication methods, 3) whether access is self-serve, trial, paid, admin-approved, or partner/contact-sales gated, 4) API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None) and breadth, 5) whether any official MCP/server exists, 6) rate limit notes. Prefer official first-party pages only. Start at https://ipayx.ai/docs and also inspect other canonical pages on ipayx.ai as needed. Return the specific page URLs visited and concise extracted evidence for each field. If docs are thin, JS-rendered, stale, contradictory, or do not support a structured field, explicitly say so.

results_truncated: {"_meta": null, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{\"data\":{\"results\":[{\"response\":{\"successful\":true,\"data\":{\"current_goal\":\"\",\"current_step\":13,\"current_url\":\"https://www.ipayx.ai/docs/api\",\"id\":\"90a979bf-61b0-4431-a54e-6f6b54f49683\",\"isSuccess\":true,\"output\":\"Based on the official iPayX documentation and site pages (ipayx.ai), here are the details:\\n\\n1) **What iPayX is:** iPayX is a forensic FX audit engine and protocol designed to expose hidden bank spreads and audit foreign exchange transactions (Source: https://www.ipayx.ai/docs/api).\\n\\n2) **Documented API Authentication Methods:** Authentication is handled via **Bearer Tokens** using an API key (e.g., `Authorization: Bearer ipx_live_...`) passed in the HTTP headers (Source: https://www.ipayx.ai/developers).\\n\\n3) **Access Type:** Access is primarily **self-serve** with tiered plans (Free, Credit Pack, Unlimited), while enterprise-grade access for banks and institutions is **contact-sales/gated** (Source: https://www.ipayx.ai/pricing).\\n\\n4) **API Surface Type & Breadth:** The API is **REST-based**. The breadth is focused on 3 core forensic endpoints: `/audit` (run audit), `/rates` (fetch historical mid-market rates), and `/audit/document` (audit PDF/image uploads) (Source: https://www.ipayx.ai/docs/api).\\n\\n5) **Official MCP/Server:** Yes, an official MCP server exists at `https://mcp.ipayx.ai/mcp` and is discoverable on major MCP directories (Source: https://www.ipayx.ai/mcp...[truncated]

raw_result.description: Forensic FX audit engine and protocol for auditing foreign exchange transactions and exposing hidden bank spreads.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official developer docs show Bearer token authentication using an API key/token in the Authorization header (example token prefix shown as ipx_live_...). No first-party evidence found for OAuth2, Basic, or separate API-key header auth.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing page lists self-serve tiers including Free, Credit Pack, and Unlimited; it also offers separate contact-sales access for banks and institutions, but a first toolkit can start on the self-serve free tier.

api_surface.breadth: Focused REST API with three documented core endpoints: /audit, /rates, and /audit/document for FX audit workflows and historical rate retrieval.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP page documents an MCP server at https://mcp.ipayx.ai/mcp.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: API docs list a 429 rate_limit_exceeded error defined as too many requests in the last 60 seconds, implying a 60-second rate-limit window; no numeric request quota was found in the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: The hinted docs URL https://ipayx.ai/docs appeared to 404, while canonical content was found under https://www.ipayx.ai/... . Public docs include developer, pricing, API, and MCP pages, suggesting active productization for both API and MCP access.
